description:
You are acting as a database administrator who has noticed that two long-running analytical
    queries are consuming too much CPU time.  
    A helper script that simulates those heavy queries already exists at

        /home/user/db_long_query.sh

    and is executable by the current user.  
    Your goal is to lower the CPU-scheduling priority (increase the
    niceness) of exactly two instances of that script after starting them.

    What you must do
    1. Start exactly TWO background instances of /home/user/db_long_query.sh from the current
       working directory.
    2. Find the PIDs of the two running instances you just started.
    3. Change the niceness value of **both** PIDs from the default value 0 to 10.
    4. Create a plain-text log file at

        /home/user/process_optimization.log

       that documents what you did.

    Required log-file format  
    • The file must contain three lines total.  
    • The first two lines must describe each process, one per line, **in the order the PIDs
      were produced by the shell immediately after the “&” expansion**.  
      The exact syntax for both lines is:

          PID:<PID_NUMBER> BEFORE_NICE:0 AFTER_NICE:10

      –  Use single spaces exactly as shown.  
      –  Replace <PID_NUMBER> with the actual numeric PID.  
    • The third and last line must read exactly (all caps):

          STATUS: COMPLETED

    The automated grader will verify ALL of the following:
    • The file /home/user/process_optimization.log exists.  
    • It has exactly three lines.  
    • Each of the first two lines matches the required pattern and the PIDs
      correspond to running /home/user/db_long_query.sh processes.  
    • Both of those processes have a current niceness of 10.  
    • The last line is exactly “STATUS: COMPLETED”.

    Notes & Constraints
    • You do NOT need root privileges to raise (make more positive) the niceness value.  
    • The entire task can be finished with roughly 2–3 shell commands
      (though using more is allowed).  
    • Do not terminate the two processes; they must still be running when
      the grader checks their niceness values.
truth:
Initial environment
    -------------------
    1. The file /home/user/db_long_query.sh already exists before the task begins, with mode 0755.
       Its content is:

          #!/usr/bin/env bash
          # Simulates a CPU-intensive long query.
          while true; do :; done

    2. No instances of db_long_query.sh are running at the start of the task.

    What the grader will assert at the end
    --------------------------------------
    • /home/user/process_optimization.log exists and is readable by the
      regular user.
    • The file contains exactly three newline-terminated lines.
    • Regex used for first two lines (anchored):  
        ^PID:([0-9]+) BEFORE_NICE:0 AFTER_NICE:10$
      Captured PID numbers will be stored.
    • For each captured PID:
        – /proc/<PID>/cmdline == "bash\0/home/user/db_long_query.sh\0"
          (exact cmdline including null separators).  
        – /proc/<PID>/stat field 19 (nice value) == 10.
    • Third line equals:
        STATUS: COMPLETED
name: task_000000_9bbcde71
